title: varronia oligodonta
intro: Varronia oligodonta, also known as the bitterbush or wild olive, is a plant species native to tropical Africa. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antimalarial, and antibacterial properties, which are attributed to the presence of various alkaloids and flavonoids. The plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, headache, and skin infections, and has also been used as a treatment for malaria and other tropical diseases. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can also be applied topically as a paste or cream to treat skin conditions.
